info.title: Pivity
info.description:
Welcome to the Pivity API! You can use our API to access Pivity API endpoints, which can be used interact with any Pivity marketplace programmatically.

# Tenant scoping
Requests to the Pivity API are not scoped to a specific tenant by default. This means you can see and interact with resources from any Pivity marketplace.
In order to only see and interact with the resources for a specific marketplace you can provide an `X-Tenant` header. Valid values for this header are:
- gmodstore.com
- rust.pivity.com

If you are using the legacy API URLs, www.gmodstore.com/api or api.gmodstore.com, you are automatically scoped to the `gmodstore.com` tenant.

# Rate limits
Every request you make to the Pivity API will count against your rate limit, which at the time of writing this, is 300 requests / minute.
An up-to-date value will always provided in the `X-RateLimit-Limit` header
The number of requests you have remaining before you must wait is provided in the `X-RateLimit-Remaining` header.

# HTTP query syntax
## The filter parameter
When using the `filter` query parameter you can pass multiple values using a comma separated list:
- `?filter[steamId]=a,b`

# Resource abilities
Some resources in the API have an `abilities` array associated with them. This array contains strings that correspond to certain actions you are allowed to perform on the given resource.

# Resource revisions
Some resources in the API have `revisionId`, `revisionCreatedAt` and `revisionsCount` fields. These fields tell you something about how many times the given resource has been edited. In some cases you are allowed to view the revision history by calling that resource's revisions endpoint.
# Validating Webhooks
Each webhook call includes the `webhook-id`, `webhook-timestamp`, and `webhook-signature` headers for verification. The `webhook-signature` header contains a space-delimited list of signatures and their corresponding version identifiers. To verify the signature, concatenate the id, timestamp, and payload separated by full-stop characters without changing the body. Use HMAC with SHA-256 and the base64 portion of your signing secret (the part after the `whsec_` prefix), which is located on the webhooks dashboard, as the key to calculate the expected signature. This should match one of the signatures in the `webhook-signature` header. Remove the version prefix and delimiter (e.g. `v1,`) before verifying the signature, and use a constant-time string comparison method to prevent timing attacks. To prevent timestamp attacks, compare the `webhook-timestamp` header against your system timestamp and ensure it's within 5 minutes.

get.summary: Fetch the current authenticated user and their access token

200.description: Successful response containing the authenticated user and their access token

post.summary: Exchange a TOTP code for a Nonce, which can in-turn be used to call protected endpoints.

requestBody.description: Response containing the nonce

200.description: Successful response

schema.description: The number of items to return per page

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: The cursor from which to return paginated results starting after

200.description: Successful response containing a list of personal access tokens

post.summary: Create a new personal access token for the current user

requestBody.description: Payload to create a new personal access token

201.description: Successful response containing the newly created personal access token, along with the plain text token

200.description: Successful response containing a single personal access token

post.summary: Create a medium for a product
post.description:
If `type` is `image` then the `image` field will be required.

If `type` is `video` then the `video` field will be required.

If `type` is `youtube` then the `youtube` field will be required.

url.description: The web address pointing to this media object

expiresAt.description: A future date less than 2 weeks from today

raw.description: Value of the money in smallest denomination of `currency` (e.g cents for USD)

amount.description: Value of the money in smallest denomination of `currency` (e.g cents for USD)

ErrorForbidden.description: The passed bearer token does not have the right scopes

ErrorNotFound.description: The requested resource does not exist

ErrorUnauthorized.description: The passed bearer token is missing or invalid

X-Ratelimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

X-Ratelimit-Reset.description: The remaining window before the rate limit resets in UTC epoch seconds.
